skill.name: service-mesh-expert
skill.description: Expert service mesh architect for Istio, Linkerd, cloud-native networking, traffic management, security policy, observability, and multi-cluster mesh design.
skill.summary: Designs and reviews service mesh architectures for Kubernetes and cloud-native microservices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported one external command pattern and three weak cryptography patterns, but review found no executable code or cryptographic implementation. The flagged text is service mesh documentation in SKILL.md, and no prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Static Finding
low_findings.description: The line references opening `resources/implementation-playbook.md` as markdown inline text. It is not Ruby backtick execution, shell execution, or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source line is plain markdown guidance inside SKILL.md. No executable script, shell syntax, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Static Findings
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain service mesh prose such as description text, workflow wording, and destination rule guidance. No DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is natural language about service mesh design. There is no code, configuration, or directive that uses a weak cryptographic primitive.

content.value_statement: Service mesh projects often fail because traffic, identity, and observability choices are made in isolation. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical mesh designs, policies, and rollout plans.
actual_capabilities: Plans Istio and Linkerd installation, configuration, and optimization work.
Designs traffic routing, load balancing, retries, circuit breakers, and canary rollout policies.
Guides mTLS setup, certificate management, authorization rules, and zero-trust network patterns.
Maps observability needs across metrics, tracing, logs, and mesh health signals.
Advises on multi-cluster and multi-cloud mesh federation patterns.
Creates operational checklists for failover testing, resilience validation, and runbook documentation.
limitations: It does not include executable automation scripts or a full implementation playbook file.
It cannot inspect a live cluster unless the host AI tool provides current cluster data.
It gives architectural guidance, but operators must validate policies in staging before production.
It does not replace vendor documentation for exact version-specific commands.

use_cases.title: Plan a First Mesh Rollout
use_cases.description: Create a staged adoption plan for adding Istio or Linkerd to existing Kubernetes services.

use_cases.title: Harden Service-to-Service Traffic
use_cases.description: Design mTLS, namespace isolation, and authorization policies for a zero-trust service model.

use_cases.title: Improve Progressive Delivery
use_cases.description: Define traffic splits, retries, circuit breakers, and observability checks for safer releases.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Mesh
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Istio and Linkerd for my Kubernetes environment. Ask for any missing constraints, then recommend a starting approach.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Rollout Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a phased service mesh rollout plan for these namespaces, traffic patterns, and reliability goals: [details].

prompt_templates.title: Review Mesh Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my intended mTLS, authorization, and namespace isolation model. Identify gaps, risks, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design Multi-Cluster Federation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-cluster service mesh architecture across these regions and failure domains: [details]. Include traffic, identity, observability, and failover guidance.

output_examples.input: We need to add a mesh to three Kubernetes namespaces with mixed HTTP and gRPC traffic.
output: A phased rollout plan starting with discovery, sidecar injection controls, and permissive mTLS.
A traffic policy checklist covering retries, timeouts, circuit breakers, and gRPC-specific health checks.
A validation plan using golden signals, distributed traces, and rollback checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Review our zero-trust approach before production enforcement.
output: A security review covering identity boundaries, namespace policies, authorization scope, and certificate rotation.
A list of enforcement risks, including permissive policy drift and missing service account ownership.
A staged path from permissive mTLS to strict mode with monitoring gates.

output_examples.input: Design cross-cluster failover for two regions.
output: A topology recommendation for service discovery, east-west gateways, and regional failover.
A resilience checklist for traffic shifting, unhealthy endpoint handling, and dependency isolation.
An operations runbook outline for drills, alerts, and recovery ownership.

best_practices: Start with clear service ownership, namespace boundaries, and observability baselines before enforcing policies.
Introduce mTLS and authorization in stages, with explicit validation gates for each namespace.
Test latency, failure handling, and rollback paths before routing production traffic through new policies.
anti_patterns: Do not enable strict mTLS everywhere before confirming service identities and dependency paths.
Do not copy traffic policies across services without checking protocol, retry safety, and timeout needs.
Do not treat mesh observability as optional, because sidecars add new failure modes.

faq.question: Which service meshes does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Istio and Linkerd, with broader guidance for Kubernetes service mesh architecture.

faq.question: Can it generate production-ready policies?
faq.answer: It can draft policy designs and validation steps, but teams must test them against their cluster and version.

faq.question: Does it require cluster access?
faq.answer: No. It can work from architecture details, manifests, and requirements supplied in the prompt.

faq.question: Can it help with multi-cluster designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers federation, cross-cluster discovery, traffic failover, and multi-cloud mesh considerations.

faq.question: Is this skill only for new deployments?
faq.answer: No. It can also review existing meshes, troubleshoot connectivity issues, and improve rollout controls.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide cluster topology, namespaces, protocols, security goals, failure domains, and current observability tools.
